economic_notes: Band owns 100% of Naha Dehe Development Corporation (business management and investment), 50% of Nahanni Butte General Stores Ltd. (general store and inn), 100% of Naha Dehe Parks Corporation (tourism infrastructure for Nahanni National Park), and 14.27% of Deh Cho Helicopters Regional Limited Partnership. Parks Canada deferred contribution of $10,000,000 on balance sheet. CMHC 7-plex project underway with $688,952 deferred. Park Trust restricted cash of $7,000,000.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to non-coterminous year end of Naha Dehe Parks Corporation (Dec 31 vs Mar 31), meaning Q1 2024 results of that entity are excluded. Province set to NT (Northwest Territories). Prior year figures were restated (Note 18) to correct unreported contribution revenue (+$148,503) and tangible capital asset composition change. Large Parks Canada deferred contribution of $10,000,000 on balance sheet. OSR calculated as total revenue $13,203,562 minus total government transfers $4,349,838 = $8,853,724, which includes deferred revenue movements and other recoveries. Annual surplus of $8,096,271 includes $89,414 earnings from subsidiaries on top of operating surplus of $8,006,857. No schedule of remuneration provided. Long-term debt appears nil based on available notes; contributions repayable to CIRNAC of $5,367 is minimal.
